Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Deep Learning Training
Short Hypothesis: A curvature-aware precision routing framework can optimize mixed-precision training by dynamically adjusting the precision of model parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape, leading to faster convergence and improved stability.
Related Work: Previous work in mixed-precision training has focused on static or heuristic-based precision assignment, which often fails to adapt to the dynamic changes in the loss landscape. Recent papers like 'Tri-Accel: Curvature-Aware Precision-Adaptive and Memory-Elastic Optimization for Efficient GPU Usage' (Sheibanian et al., 2025) have explored adaptive precision but lack a formal curvature-aware routing mechanism. Our proposal introduces a deterministic, curvature-aware routing protocol that formalizes stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, providing a more rigorous and transparent approach.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework for deep learning training. This framework dynamically adjusts the precision of model parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape, ensuring numerical stability and efficient resource utilization. By integrating spectral quantization theory and deterministic spectral estimators, we formalize stability radii and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our approach guarantees strict overhead bounds and preserves deterministic execution pathways, making it suitable for single-GPU deployments. Empirical validation on CIFAR-10 and ResNet-18 demonstrates significant improvements in training time and memory usage while maintaining or improving accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the curvature-aware precision routing framework on CIFAR-10 using ResNet-18.
Experiments.Method: Train ResNet-18 with the proposed framework, dynamically adjusting precision based on the curvature of the loss landscape. Compare against static mixed-precision and full-precision baselines.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, accuracy, and stability (measured by gradient variance and loss convergence rate).

Experiments.Description: Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of curvature-aware routing.
Experiments.Method: Train ResNet-18 with and without curvature-aware routing, using the same computational resources and hyperparameters.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, and accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Validate the framework on a more complex dataset and model.
Experiments.Method: Train EfficientNet-B0 on CIFAR-100 using the proposed framework and compare against baselines.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, and accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Perform multi-seed variance quantification to ensure reproducibility.
Experiments.Method: Run multiple training runs with different random seeds and report the mean and standard deviation of training time, memory usage, and accuracy.
Experiments.Metrics: Mean and standard deviation of training time, memory usage, and accuracy.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The curvature estimation might be computationally expensive, which could offset the gains in training efficiency.
The framework's effectiveness might vary across different models and datasets, requiring further validation.
The deterministic routing protocol might introduce overhead in the form of pre-execution diagnostics, which could affect overall performance.
